topics.title: Europe's heatwave turns more severe
topics.summary: A record-setting European heatwave has brought extreme temperatures, transport disruption and health warnings, with France reporting around 1,000 additional deaths as the heat shifts east.
topics.discussionPrompt: When heat starts affecting trains, power, rivers and daily routines, does summer feel more like something to plan around than simply enjoy?

topics.title: DR Congo earns historic World Cup win
topics.summary: DR Congo beat Uzbekistan 3-1 for its first World Cup victory, advancing to the knockout stage and setting up a round-of-32 match against England.
topics.discussionPrompt: Do you enjoy seeing new soccer nations make a splash, or do you prefer the familiar giants dominating the big tournaments?

topics.title: Messi extends his World Cup scoring record
topics.summary: Lionel Messi became the first player to score in seven straight World Cup matches, adding another milestone with a free kick for Argentina against Jordan.
topics.discussionPrompt: At what point does a sports record become part of the player’s legend, even for people who do not usually follow the sport?

topics.title: Supreme Court nears major rulings
topics.summary: The U.S. Supreme Court is nearing the end of its term, with major pending decisions on presidential power, elections, school sports rules and geofence warrants.
topics.discussionPrompt: Court decisions can sound abstract until they touch daily life. Which area feels more important to watch: elections, privacy, or government power?

topics.title: BET Awards prepare for live broadcast
topics.summary: The 2026 BET Awards are set for tonight in Los Angeles, with Druski hosting, Cardi B leading nominations and special honors planned for Lauryn Hill and Teyana Taylor.
topics.discussionPrompt: Award shows are part music, part fashion and part cultural snapshot. Do you watch for the winners, the performances, or the unexpected moments?
